frame_analyses.description: White background with no instruments visible yet
Yellow Penrose drain with slit visible, needle driver approaching with loaded needle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears properly loaded on driver, good positioning

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver positioning for entry through first mark on drain
Suture being pulled through drain, black suture material visible
Hands visible forming knot extracorporeally with suture tails
Continued knot formation with multiple wraps visible
Knot formation continuing, suture management appears controlled
Knot being advanced toward tissue using pusher technique
Knot pusher being used to advance knot to tissue level
Scissors visible preparing to cut suture tails

knot_assessments.notes: Well-formed extracorporeal knot with appropriate tension

confidence_rationale: Task appears to be Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture) based on visible extracorporeal knot formation, but labeled as Task 5. Video quality and angles make precise penalty assessment difficult. Suture placement accuracy cannot be definitively determined from available frames.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at suture entry points due to camera angle and resolution
Whether this is actually Task 5 (intracorporeal) vs Task 4 (extracorporeal) - technique shown appears extracorporeal
Precise knot security assessment without stress testing visibility
technique_summary: Excellent technical performance with expert-level timing in most phases. Clean execution of extracorporeal suturing technique with appropriate instrument handling and knot formation.
strengths: Smooth needle loading and suture placement
improvement_suggestions: Confirm task type - technique appears more consistent with Task 4 extracorporeal suturing
Ensure proper intracorporeal technique if Task 5 is intended
